# nilql

Library for working with encrypted data within nilDB queries and replies.

{{ actions }}

## Installation and Usage

The library can be imported in the usual ways:

```python
import nilql
from nilql import *
```

## Development

All installation and development dependencies are fully specified in `pyproject.toml`. The `project.optional-dependencies` object is used to [specify optional requirements](https://peps.python.org/pep-0621) for various development tasks. This makes it possible to specify additional options (such as `docs`, `lint`, and so on) when performing installation using [pip](https://pypi.org/project/pip):

```bash
python -m pip install ".[docs,lint]"
```

### Documentation

The documentation can be generated automatically from the source files using [Sphinx](https://www.sphinx-doc.org):

```bash
python -m pip install ".[docs]"
cd docs
sphinx-apidoc -f -E --templatedir=_templates -o _source .. && make html
```

### Testing and Conventions

All unit tests are executed and their coverage is measured when using [pytest](https://docs.pytest.org) (see the `pyproject.toml` file for configuration details):

```bash
python -m pip install ".[test]"
python -m pytest
```

The subset of the unit tests included in the module itself and can be executed using [doctest](https://docs.python.org/3/library/doctest.html):

```bash
python src/nilql/nilql.py -v
```

Style conventions are enforced using [Pylint](https://pylint.readthedocs.io):

```bash
python -m pip install ".[lint]"
python -m pylint src/nilql test/test_nilql.py
```

### Contributions

In order to contribute to the source code, open an issue or submit a pull request on the [GitHub page](https://github.com/nillionnetwork/nilql-py) for this library.

### Versioning

The version number format for this library and the changes to the library associated with version number increments conform with [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0](https://semver.org/#semantic-versioning-200).

modules.name: nilql
documentation: Allow users to access the functions directly.

modules.name: nilql.nilql
documentation:
Python library for working with encrypted data within nilDB queries and
replies.

functions.name: nilql.nilql._encode
documentation:
Encode a numeric value or string as a byte array. The encoding includes
information about the type of the value (to enable decoding without any
additional context).

```pycon
>>> _encode(123).hex()
'007b00008000000000'
>>> _encode('abc').hex()
'01616263'
```

If a value cannot be encoded, an exception is raised.

```pycon
>>> _encode([1, 2, 3])
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
ValueError: cannot encode value
```

functions.name: nilql.nilql._decode
documentation:
Decode a bytes-like object back into a numeric value or string.

```pycon
>>> _decode(_encode(123))
123
>>> _decode(_encode('abc'))
'abc'
```

If a value cannot be decoded, an exception is raised.

```pycon
>>> _decode([1, 2, 3])
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
TypeError: can only decode bytes-like object
>>> _decode(bytes([2]))
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
ValueError: cannot decode value
```

functions.name: nilql.nilql.secret_key
documentation:
Return a secret key built according to what is specified in the supplied
cluster configuration and operation list.

functions.name: nilql.nilql.encrypt
documentation:
Return the ciphertext obtained by using the supplied key to encrypt the
supplied plaintext.

```pycon
>>> key = secret_key({'nodes': [{}]}, {'store': True})
>>> isinstance(encrypt(key, 123), bytes)
True
```

functions.name: nilql.nilql.decrypt
documentation:
Return the ciphertext obtained by using the supplied key to encrypt the
supplied plaintext.

```pycon
>>> key = secret_key({'nodes': [{}, {}]}, {'store': True})
>>> decrypt(key, encrypt(key, 123))
123
>>> key = secret_key({'nodes': [{}, {}]}, {'store': True})
>>> decrypt(key, encrypt(key, -10))
-10
>>> key = secret_key({'nodes': [{}]}, {'store': True})
>>> decrypt(key, encrypt(key, 'abc'))
'abc'
>>> key = secret_key({'nodes': [{}]}, {'store': True})
>>> decrypt(key, encrypt(key, 123))
123
>>> key = secret_key({'nodes': [{}, {}]}, {'sum': True})
>>> decrypt(key, encrypt(key, 123))
123
>>> key = secret_key({'nodes': [{}, {}]}, {'sum': True})
>>> decrypt(key, encrypt(key, -10))
-10
```

If a value cannot be decrypted, an exception is raised.

```pycon
>>> key = secret_key({'nodes': [{}, {}]}, {'abc': True})
>>> decrypt(key, encrypt(key, [1, 2, 3]))
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
ValueError: cannot decrypt supplied ciphertext using the supplied key
```

variables.name: nilql.nilql._PLAINTEXT_SIGNED_INTEGER_MIN
documentation: Minimum plaintext 32-bit signed integer value that can be encrypted.

variables.name: nilql.nilql._PLAINTEXT_SIGNED_INTEGER_MAX
documentation: Maximum plaintext 32-bit signed integer value that can be encrypted.

variables.name: nilql.nilql._SECRET_SHARED_SIGNED_INTEGER_MODULUS
documentation: Modulus to use for additive secret sharing of 32-bit signed integers.

variables.name: nilql.nilql._PLAINTEXT_STRING_BUFFER_LEN_MAX
documentation: Maximum length of plaintext string values that can be encrypted.
